workflow.name: ACH Disbursement with Returns
workflow.description:
#UC2: ACH Disbursement Processing

Records the sub-ledger accounting for customer disbursements via the ACH rail, including returns handling and Suspense-based reconciliation.

## Phase 1 -- Disbursement Initiation (Step 1)

Customer funds are reclassified from Available (20100) to Pending Disbursements (20102) with FDIC tracking mirrors.

## Phase 2 -- ACH File Creation (Steps 2-3)

The payment gateway assembles a NACHA file. The batch header (T5203/Step 3) establishes the per-file Clearing Files (10104) balance. Per-customer detail lines (T5204/Step 2) draw down the per-file balance and zero the customer's Pending Disbursements.

## Phase 3 -- Returns Handling (Steps 4-6)

ACH returns arrive as separate files. The return batch header (T5208/Step 5) establishes a per-file Clearing Files (10104) balance for the return file. Per-customer return detail lines (T5207/Step 4) draw down the per-file balance and park in Unallocated Customer (20900). The reconciliation service (T5209/Step 6) matches returns to customers and moves to Hold (20101).

## Phase 4 -- Bank Statement (Step 7)

The bank statement confirms the net cash movement on the FBO. Creates Control (10102) balance.

## Phase 5 -- Suspense-Based Reconciliation (Steps 8-10)

Three reconciliation templates zero out all temporary accounts:
- T5210 (Step 8): Flush disbursement clearing (10101 -> 10103) per customer
- T5211 (Step 9): Flush return clearing (10103 -> 10101) and release customer Hold to Available, per return
- T5212 (Step 10): Flush control (10103 -> 10102) per bank statement match

**Final state:** All clearing, control, and suspense accounts net to zero. Customer Available decreased by net disbursements minus returns. Cash (10100) decreased by the bank statement amount.

steps.description: Move customer funds from Available to Pending Disbursements, queuing them for inclusion in the next NACHA file. Liability-only -- no house assets touched until the file is created.

Record a customer's entry in the NACHA file, drawing down their Pending Disbursements balance. This is the anchor event for the disbursement lifecycle -- a T5210 child means the line has been reconciled.
Establish the expected total for this NACHA file. The per-file clearing balance acts as a loading checksum -- it must reach zero once every customer detail line has loaded.

Load a single ACH return into the unallocated staging area. This is the anchor event for the return lifecycle -- children T5209 (allocation) and T5211 (release) track it through matching and customer release.
Establish the expected total for this ACH return file. Same checksum pattern as the disbursement file -- per-file balance must reach zero when all return lines have loaded.
Match a returned ACH entry to a specific customer and move it from unallocated staging to that customer's hold position. Funds go to Hold pending reconciliation and release.
Confirm that cash has physically left the FBO bank account. Creates a per-batch control position for later reconciliation against the disbursement and return clearing sides.
Reconcile a single disbursement line by moving its clearing position into suspense. Fires per detail line matched by the reconciliation service.
Reconcile a return and release the customer's funds from Hold back to Available, completing the return lifecycle. Fires per return line matched by the reconciliation service.
Pair the bank statement against the clearing and return sides by flushing control through suspense. Completes the reconciliation cycle -- all temporary accounts net to zero.
